nodes.label: A Google Ads Targeting Tactic That Cut Invalid Clicks by 50%

nodes.author: John Horn
nodes.norm_label: a google ads targeting tactic that cut invalid clicks by 50%

nodes.label: Invalid Clicks / Click Fraud
nodes.source_file: Clippings/A Google Ads targeting tactic that cut invalid clicks by 50%.md
nodes.source_location: ## What click fraud and 'invalid clicks' actually are
nodes.author: John Horn

nodes.label: Predefined-Audience 'Targeting' Tactic
nodes.rationale: Fraudulent/bot traffic cycling through IPs and VPNs typically doesn't build the browsing history needed to land in Google's predefined demographic/interest audiences, so restricting delivery to users who fall into any such audience (set to 'Targeting', not 'Observation') filters out a large share of that traffic even without targeting a specific audience.
nodes.source_file: Clippings/A Google Ads targeting tactic that cut invalid clicks by 50%.md

nodes.author: John Horn

nodes.label: How I Built a Second Brain That Maintains Itself

nodes.norm_label: how i built a second brain that maintains itself

nodes.label: LLM Wiki Pattern (Three Layers: Raw Sources / Wiki / Schema)
nodes.rationale: Treats knowledge as a compounding asset rather than a retrieval problem: raw sources are immutable, the AI-owned wiki layer accumulates cross-referenced pages, and a schema file (CLAUDE.md) tells the AI the conventions/workflows to follow, so maintenance cost stays near zero.

nodes.label: Proportionate Response Framework
nodes.rationale: Direct trademark use in ad copy should escalate through Google's trademark complaint process and then legal counsel; modifier bidding, comparison pages, and DKI are generally not policy violations, so they're better addressed through PPC/SEO strategy — and the cost of any response should stay below the value of the branded traffic being protected.

nodes.source_location: ### 2. Build a prompt library instead of a keyword list
nodes.author: Casey Nifong

nodes.label: 5-Step Framework for Tracking AI Visibility
nodes.rationale: AI search visibility is probabilistic, not deterministic (no 'position 1'), so measurement should shift from rank-tracking to prompt-cluster inclusion rate, multi-turn conversation coverage, and framing/sentiment, since no platform exposes every AI conversation.
nodes.source_file: Clippings/How to measure prompt-level visibility in AI search.md

nodes.label: LLM Knowledge Bases Claim
nodes.rationale: Index raw sources into raw/, then have an LLM incrementally compile a linked markdown wiki (summaries, concepts, backlinks) that can answer complex queries without needing to reach for RAG at small-to-medium scale; 'Every business has a raw/ directory. Nobody's ever compiled it. That's the product.'

nodes.label: lead_status Field Unpopulated Gap
nodes.rationale: CallRail v3 documents lead_status as the Sales sub-agent's primary lead-qualification signal, but the live June 2026 audit found it unset on 100% of 370 calls — needs a decision: start manually tagging it, or design qualification logic around what's actually populated (tags + lead_score) instead.
nodes.source_location: ## Gaps found (flagged, not yet resolved), item 1

nodes.label: value Field Unpopulated Gap
nodes.rationale: value is unset on all 370 audited calls — no revenue is being attributed through CallRail, so any claim assuming CallRail-sourced revenue numbers (e.g. in Recommendations) isn't backed by real data yet.

nodes.label: lead_status field
nodes.rationale: Documented as CallRail's primary lead-qualification signal, but confirmed 0% populated in the live Shumaker Roofing account as of July 2026 — not actually usable despite being the documented default.

nodes.label: value / formatted_value field
nodes.rationale: Documented monetary value field, also confirmed 0% populated live — same gap as lead_status.

nodes.label: tags field
nodes.rationale: Not the documented primary signal, but the field actually populated and used live: ~24% of audited June 2026 calls carry a Schedule requested/booked tag.

nodes.label: lead_score field (Premium CI)
nodes.rationale: AI-graded lead score, populated on 69% of audited calls (avg 39.6/100) — along with tags, the real working qualification signal despite lead_status/value being the documented ones.

nodes.label: Two-Tier Retrieval
nodes.source_file: Project Jarvis/Layer 6 - Skills and Knowledge Injection.md
nodes.rationale: Mirrors how Hermes and Claude Skills work: always-on core memory for zero-retrieval-latency authoritative definitions, plus on-demand vector retrieval for larger corpora, so sub-agents never hallucinate business logic.

nodes.label: Always-on Core Memory
nodes.source_file: Project Jarvis/Layer 6 - Skills and Knowledge Injection.md

nodes.label: Anti-Hallucination Patterns
nodes.source_file: Project Jarvis/Layer 6 - Skills and Knowledge Injection.md
nodes.rationale: Inject definitions rather than letting the model infer them; require sub-agents to cite the source file/field for business-logic claims; compute metrics from raw figures + canonical formula file rather than estimating; keep the 'qualified lead' definition identical across Marketing and Sales agents.

nodes.label: Text-to-SQL Injection/Write Risk
nodes.rationale: Carries the highest blast radius of the five connectors (raw DB access), so it is isolated from day one and shipped first as the MVP to prove the guardrail stack before any other system is touched.

nodes.label: Worked Example: Victor's Pipeline vs Google Ads Spend
nodes.norm_label: worked example: victor's pipeline vs google ads spend

nodes.label: Prompt Injection Prevention
nodes.rationale: Treat retrieved content as untrusted data never instructions; use structured/parameterized tool calls; validate model-proposed arguments against a schema — MCP-schema contract engines report blocking ~98% of unsafe queries at ~8–13ms overhead.

nodes.label: Defense in Depth Principle
nodes.rationale: Assume injection will eventually succeed; rely on least-privilege tool scopes and read-only roles to bound damage; a guardrail LLM is itself injectable and is one layer, not the whole defense.

nodes.label: AccuLynx 'Dealbreaker' Is False (Key Finding)
nodes.rationale: AccuLynx publishes a documented public REST API v2 with Bearer API-key auth and read endpoints for everything the Sales sub-agent needs; real constraints are per-location keys, a 10 req/sec/key rate limit, and write-capable keys that must be locked down.

nodes.label: Key Finding: Connector Feasibility Better Than Assumed
nodes.norm_label: key finding: connector feasibility better than assumed

nodes.label: Key Finding: Heterogeneous Auth Must Be Abstracted
nodes.rationale: OAuth2 three-legged flows with 24-hour token rotation (Google Ads, GHL v2) alongside static Bearer/API-key auth (AccuLynx, CallRail) argue strongly for wrapping each platform in its own MCP server that owns credential lifecycle.
nodes.norm_label: key finding: heterogeneous auth must be abstracted

nodes.label: Key Finding: Read-only Is Enforceable But Not Free
nodes.rationale: Several APIs issue write-capable keys by default with no read-only scope, so read-only must be enforced at the database role, tool-allowlist, and prompt-guardrail layers — defense in depth.
nodes.norm_label: key finding: read-only is enforceable but not free

nodes.label: Key Finding: Claude Agent SDK Natively Implements Hub-and-Spoke
nodes.norm_label: key finding: claude agent sdk natively implements hub-and-spoke

nodes.label: Key Finding: Voice Is a Bolt-on, Not a Foundation
nodes.norm_label: key finding: voice is a bolt-on, not a foundation

nodes.label: Project Jarvis: Multi-Agent AI Architecture PDF
nodes.source_file: Clippings/Project Jarvis_ Multi-Agent AI Architecture for Shumaker Roofing.pdf
nodes.norm_label: project jarvis: multi-agent ai architecture pdf

nodes.label: Claude Agent SDK Recommendation
nodes.rationale: Chosen as the harness because the orchestrator→sub-agent topology maps 1:1 onto its native subagents (isolated context, per-agent allowedTools, Task-tool delegation), and every connector plugs in as an MCP server — trade-off accepted: locks the system to Claude models and runs via a CLI subprocess.
nodes.source_file: Clippings/Project Jarvis_ Multi-Agent AI Architecture for Shumaker Roofing.pdf

nodes.label: Cloud VPS Hosting Recommendation (Hetzner/DigitalOcean)
nodes.rationale: Recommended primary host for a read-only, API-driven agent that never needs a local GUI — no hardware dependency, trivial remote access, systemd auto-restart; Mac Mini is only justified for iMessage or local browser/screen automation, Replit only for prototyping.
nodes.source_file: Clippings/Project Jarvis_ Multi-Agent AI Architecture for Shumaker Roofing.pdf

nodes.label: Dedicated Mac Mini Option
nodes.source_file: Clippings/Project Jarvis_ Multi-Agent AI Architecture for Shumaker Roofing.pdf

nodes.label: AccuLynx 'Dealbreaker' Debunked
nodes.rationale: AccuLynx publishes a documented public REST API v2 (Bearer auth) with read endpoints for Jobs, Milestones, Financials, Invoices, Payments, Sales Owner, and Users — everything the Sales sub-agent needs. Real constraints are per-location API keys, a 10 req/sec/key rate limit, zero developer support, and write-capable keys with no read-only scope.
nodes.source_file: Clippings/Project Jarvis_ Multi-Agent AI Architecture for Shumaker Roofing.pdf

nodes.label: 2-Agent vs 3-Agent Topology Recommendation
nodes.rationale: Start with combined Marketing+Sales (they share the GCLID/UTM→lead→job attribution spine) plus an isolated Reporting agent (highest blast radius, raw DB access). Promote to 3 agents when the combined agent's tool list exceeds ~20-25 tools or routing accuracy degrades.
nodes.source_file: Clippings/Project Jarvis_ Multi-Agent AI Architecture for Shumaker Roofing.pdf

nodes.source_file: Clippings/Project Jarvis_ Multi-Agent AI Architecture for Shumaker Roofing.pdf

nodes.label: CallRail v3 Connector
nodes.source_file: Clippings/Project Jarvis_ Multi-Agent AI Architecture for Shumaker Roofing.pdf

nodes.label: PostgreSQL Reporting Connector
nodes.source_file: Clippings/Project Jarvis_ Multi-Agent AI Architecture for Shumaker Roofing.pdf

nodes.label: Defense-in-Depth Read-Only Enforcement
nodes.rationale: Read-only must be enforced at three layers simultaneously — the database role (GRANT SELECT only), the MCP tool-allowlist (only GET tools ever implemented), and the prompt/guardrail layer — because several vendor APIs (e.g. AccuLynx) issue keys that are write-capable by default with no read-only scope.
nodes.source_file: Clippings/Project Jarvis_ Multi-Agent AI Architecture for Shumaker Roofing.pdf

nodes.label: Prompt Injection Mitigation Strategy
nodes.rationale: Treat all retrieved content (transcripts, CRM notes) as untrusted data never as instructions; validate every model-proposed tool argument against a schema before execution; assume injection eventually succeeds and rely on least-privilege read-only scopes to bound the damage; audit every tool call.
nodes.source_file: Clippings/Project Jarvis_ Multi-Agent AI Architecture for Shumaker Roofing.pdf

nodes.rationale: Compelling for the channel layer (bridges Slack/iMessage/Telegram with persistent cross-platform memory) but its always-accumulating self-improving memory is a prompt-injection surface that shouldn't write business-logic memory unsupervised in a finance-adjacent system.
